name: Freight Density (pounds per cubic foot)
description: Shipment density in pounds per cubic foot (PCF): total cubic feet from inch dimensions and piece count, then actual weight divided by that volume. Density is the primary input to LTL freight-class evaluation under the NMFC density provisions.

inputs.name: Length
inputs.description: Longest side of the handling unit, including packaging and pallet.

inputs.name: Height
inputs.description: Measure to the highest point, including the pallet height.

inputs.name: Pieces
inputs.description: Number of identical handling units; use 1 for a single pallet or crate.

inputs.name: Actual weight
inputs.description: Total scale weight of all pieces, including packaging and pallet.

outputs.name: Total volume
outputs.description: Total cubic feet of the shipment (1 ft3 = 1,728 in3).

outputs.name: Density
outputs.description: Pounds per cubic foot — the figure compared against the NMFC density scale.

expression: V = (L * W * H * Q) / 1728; PCF = AW / V
assumptions: Dimensions are outer dimensions of the handling unit as shipped, including packaging and pallet.
All pieces share the same dimensions; weight is the combined scale weight.
1 cubic foot = 1,728 cubic inches (12 x 12 x 12).
limitations: Density is only one classification factor — the NMFC also weighs handling, stowability, and liability, and any of them can move a commodity off its density-implied class.
Many commodities carry fixed (non-density) NMFC classes; density alone does not determine their class.
Carriers may measure and round dimensions themselves; a reweigh/re-measure can change the billed density.

examples.note: One 48 x 40 x 48 in pallet weighing 500 lb: V = 92,160 / 1728 = 53.33 ft3; PCF = 500 / 53.33 = 9.375 lb/ft3.

citations.quote: CUBIC_FEET | Cubic feet from inches | `(length_in x width_in x height_in) / 1728` | inches | ft3
`weight_lb / cubic_feet` | lb, ft3 | lb/ft3 | Use measured handling unit including packaging/pallet where applicable | LTL freight class density evaluation
